description:
## Background and intent

The earlier beads can all pass in isolation while the actual desktop user path still fails: Rust may parse fixtures, TauRPC may compile, Effect may map states, and React may render, but Beadsmith might still fail to show real Beadwork issues when launched from a workspace. This bead must provide the user-level proof for the first Issue List slice by running a true WebDriver end-to-end test against the desktop app.

The goal is a repeatable WebDriver end-to-end test with useful logging that a future agent can run before closing the epic. It must make failures diagnosable: which workspace was used, whether `bw` was found, what command path was exercised, and what UI state appeared.

## What to build

Add a WebDriver end-to-end test that launches the Beadsmith Tauri desktop app against a small deterministic Beadwork workspace and verifies the issue explorer renders real `IssueSummary` data through the full Rust adapter -> TauRPC -> Effect -> React path. This is not a renderer-only test, mocked browser-mode test, manual checklist, or service-level script; it must automate the desktop app through WebDriver.

The test fixture must include at least one issue with labels/dependencies or optional metadata, plus one scenario that produces a true empty list or a controlled read failure. Logs must identify the workspace path, command/result state, and UI state being asserted.


## WebDriver/Tauri implementation guidance

Use Tauri's WebDriver path as the required end-to-end route for this bead. The Tauri testing docs recommend WebdriverIO with `@wdio/tauri-service` for desktop automation on Windows, Linux, and macOS. Prefer the service's default `embedded` driver provider for cross-platform local runs, especially macOS, because official `tauri-driver` only supports Windows/Linux when driven directly. If using `external`/official `tauri-driver`, document the platform limitation and wrap Linux CI commands with `xvfb-run` rather than relying on WDIO `autoXvfb`.

Concrete setup notes for Beadsmith:

- Add a WDIO config such as `wdio.issue-list.conf.ts` with `services: [['@wdio/tauri-service', { driverProvider: 'embedded' }]]`, one Tauri capability, and `wdio:tauriServiceOptions` pointing at the built Beadsmith binary.
- Pass the deterministic Beadwork workspace into the app via the same path the product will use at runtime: app args, environment variable, or configured workspace location. The spec should log the workspace path and should not bake in machine-specific absolute paths.
- Enable backend/frontend log capture in `wdio:tauriServiceOptions` so the WebDriver output includes Rust/TauRPC/Effect/UI signals when the assertion fails.
- For the embedded provider, wire the app side with the WDIO plugins: add `tauri-plugin-wdio` for `browser.tauri.execute()`, command mocking, and log capture; add `tauri-plugin-wdio-webdriver` for the embedded WebDriver server; grant `wdio:default` and `wdio-webdriver:default` permissions in the Tauri capability.
- In the spec, assert both DOM rendering and one native command path. Use normal WDIO DOM queries for the issue list, and use `browser.tauri.execute(({ core }) => core.invoke(...))` only for diagnostics or direct command sanity checks; the primary success assertion should still prove the user-visible issue explorer rendered real `IssueSummary` data.
- Browser-mode WDIO tests are only acceptable as supplemental coverage. Browser mode can mock/intercept `invoke()`, but it does not launch the Tauri binary and therefore cannot satisfy this bead's full Rust adapter -> TauRPC -> Effect -> React requirement by itself.

Reference evidence from the WebdriverIO Tauri example:

- The fixture exposes `pnpm test` as `wdio run ./wdio.conf.ts` and builds a debug Tauri binary before testing: https://github.com/webdriverio/desktop-mobile/blob/0d566c99021f12efbc5cb028c43f1ce049163e0f/fixtures/e2e-apps/tauri/package.json#L6-L14
- The Tauri app includes both `tauri-plugin-wdio` and `tauri-plugin-wdio-webdriver`: https://github.com/webdriverio/desktop-mobile/blob/0d566c99021f12efbc5cb028c43f1ce049163e0f/fixtures/e2e-apps/tauri/src-tauri/Cargo.toml#L14-L21
- Its capability grants `wdio:default` and `wdio-webdriver:default`: https://github.com/webdriverio/desktop-mobile/blob/0d566c99021f12efbc5cb028c43f1ce049163e0f/fixtures/e2e-apps/tauri/src-tauri/capabilities/default.json#L9-L16
- The Rust entry point always installs `tauri_plugin_wdio`, and conditionally installs the embedded WebDriver plugin when `WDIO_EMBEDDED_SERVER` is set: https://github.com/webdriverio/desktop-mobile/blob/0d566c99021f12efbc5cb028c43f1ce049163e0f/fixtures/e2e-apps/tauri/src-tauri/src/main.rs#L276-L286
- The WDIO config pattern enables backend/frontend log capture in `wdio:tauriServiceOptions`: https://github.com/webdriverio/desktop-mobile/blob/0d566c99021f12efbc5cb028c43f1ce049163e0f/fixtures/package-tests/tauri-app/wdio.conf.ts#L54-L68
- The service allocates driver ports dynamically; do not hardcode a port in the app-level config: https://github.com/webdriverio/desktop-mobile/blob/0d566c99021f12efbc5cb028c43f1ce049163e0f/fixtures/package-tests/tauri-app/wdio.conf.ts#L72-L104
- Example specs call Tauri commands through `browser.tauri.execute(({ core }) => core.invoke(...))`: https://github.com/webdriverio/desktop-mobile/blob/0d566c99021f12efbc5cb028c43f1ce049163e0f/e2e/test/tauri/api.spec.ts#L4-L18

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] A repeatable WebDriver end-to-end command exists for the completed Issue List slice.
- [ ] The WebDriver test verifies that at least one real Beadwork issue appears in the issue explorer when launched against a deterministic workspace.
- [ ] The verification path exercises the approved integration stack: Rust adapter, TauRPC boundary, Effect service, and React rendering.
- [ ] The WebDriver test emits detailed logs for workspace setup, `bw` availability/path, app launch or service invocation, and assertions.
- [ ] Empty or failure-state coverage is included in the WebDriver test.
- [ ] The WebDriver end-to-end command is documented where future agents can find it.
- [ ] The test does not require committing local machine-specific paths.
- [ ] Relevant checks plus the new WebDriver end-to-end command pass before closing the epic.

## Out of scope

- Full regression coverage for future workspace switching, search, filters, issue detail, editing, or mutations.
- Replacing focused unit tests from earlier beads.

## Triage

ready-for-agent

comments.text: Planning audit: added because isolated unit/contract/render tests can all pass while the actual desktop user path still fails. This final bead should prove the complete adapter -> TauRPC -> Effect -> React flow against a deterministic Beadwork workspace with useful logs for future debugging.

title: Add WebDriver end-to-end coverage for the Issue List slice
